issues.title: If the MCP server defines content path, the client endpoint can pass the first GET request sent to the/se path, but sending a post request with the response/mcp/message is incorrect, resulting in a 404 error
issues.body:
**Bug description**
How does MCP Server define content path and connect to URL: localhost: port sse endpoint:/contentPath/sse using SSE after defining it? This format can be used for the first get request to connect, but for the second post request to access/mcp/message, the contentpath cannot be added, resulting in a 404 error

**Environment**
springboot:3.4.2  jdk 17 spring-ai 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

**Steps to reproduce**
    mcp:
      client:
        enabled: true
        name: mock-client
        version: 0.0.1
        type: SYNC
        request-timeout: 20s
        sse:
          connections:
            soldier-server:
              url: http://localhost:port
              sse-endpoint: /content-path/sse

issues.title: Issues with models not maintained by Spring AI
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested.

**Expected Behavior**

<!--- Tell us how it should work. Add a code example to explain what you think the feature should look like. This is optional, but it would help up understand your expectations. -->

**Current Behavior**

<!--- Explain the difference from current behavior and why do you need this feature (aka why it is not possible to implement the desired functionality with the current version) -->

**Context**

Will there be models like [moonshot](https://github.com/spring-ai-community/moonshot) or [qianfan](https://github.com/spring-ai-community/qianfan)
that are not maintained by Spring AI in the future? Some dependency names are not unified, which makes integration very inconvenient.

comments.body:
The Spring AI Community project, which we will be announcing at the launch of Spring AI 1.0 GA is where these and other project will live.

https://github.com/spring-ai-community/moonshot and https://github.com/spring-ai-community/qianfan are already there, though the package names and the publishing out to maven central still needs to be sorted. out.

issues.title: Issue Report: Inconsistency in Spring AI MCP Client documentation regarding default value of toolcallback.enabled
issues.body:
# Issue Report: Inconsistency in Spring AI MCP Client documentation regarding default value of `toolcallback.enabled`

## Issue Description

I've identified a documentation inconsistency in the Spring AI documentation regarding the default value of the `spring.ai.mcp.client.toolcallback.enabled` property.

### Conflicting Information

In the [MCP Client Boot Starter documentation](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_usage_example), there are two conflicting statements:

1. **First statement indicates default value is `true`**:
   ```
   Property: toolcallback.enabled
   Description: Enable/disable the MCP tool callback integration with Spring AI's tool execution framework
   Default Value: true
   ```

2. **Second statement indicates default value is `false`**:
   ```
   Note that the tool callback functionality is disabled by default and must be explicitly enabled with:
   spring:
     ai:
       mcp:
         client:
           toolcallback:
             enabled: true
   ```

## Impact

This inconsistency makes it unclear whether the tool callback functionality is enabled or disabled by default, leading to potential confusion for developers using the Spring AI MCP Client.

## Additional Information

- Spring AI Version: [version number]
- Documentation URL: https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_usage_example
- Date observed: [current date]

## Suggested Fix

Update the documentation to provide consistent information about the default value of `toolcallback.enabled`. If the actual default value is `false`, then the properties table should be updated to reflect this. If the default value is `true`, then the note about explicit enabling should be removed or clarified.

## Question

Could you confirm the actual default value for `spring.ai.mcp.client.toolcallback.enabled` in the current implementation?

Thank you for your attention to this matter and for maintaining the Spring AI documentation.

comments.body: Yes, Spring AI is at 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
Can you point to the reproducible example that you have please in github to make this easier to triage.

The code is part of a larger application, which can not be run without configuring database and other dependent servers.

I have shared the repo privately with you @markpollack
The latest changes in Spring AI in the snapshot have introduced this issue, as I reverted to my last commit

@markpollack 
problem is due to a conflict in the ./m2 which had multiple versions of MCP SDK and 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT was getting into a conflict.

Cleared ./m2/../io/modelcontextprotocol and that resolves it. 
There was a similar issue on M7 --> M8

issues.title: An error occurred while using springai to call the local vllm model. It has been confirmed that vllm is using the OpenAI compatibility mode.
issues.body:
**Bug description**
调用本地vllm中Qwen3-32B-AWQ报错

**Environment**
Please provide as many details as possible: Spring AI version, Java version, which vector store you use if any, etc

**Steps to reproduce**
1.0.0-M8,jdk17,redis-stack

**Expected behavior**
An error occurred (400 Bad Request) when calling the locally hosted OpenAI-compatible vllm model through springai.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**

你启动vLLM程序的时候有没有加 `--enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser hermes` 这个参数，大模型调用工具是要加这个参数的。  
我第一次的时候也遇到400错误了，不知道和你遇到的是不是同一个问题。  
我用 Wireshark 抓包的时候意外发现响应体里面写了错误原因，缺了两个启动参数造成的，Spring AI并没有把这个错误消息抛出来。 
 
完整的启动命令是这样的，我是Qwen2.5，用了Docker：  
```shell
docker run --runtime nvidia --gpus all -p 8000:8000 --env "HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1" --ipc=host \
  -v "E:\AI\LLM\Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Q8_0:/models/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Q8_0" \
  --name=qwen2.5-7b-q8 vllm/vllm-openai:latest \
  --model /models/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Q8_0/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Q8_0.gguf \
  --tokenizer /models/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Q8_0 \
  --served_model_name qwen2.5-7b \
  --max_model_len 4096 --quantization gguf --api_key "123456" \
  --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser hermes \
  --uvicorn-log-level trace
```

The project configuration is as follows:

spring:
>ai:
>>zhipuai:
>>>api-key: myKey
>>>>chat:
>>>>>options:
>>>>>>model: glm-4-flash-250414

>>mcp:
>>>client:
>>>>enabled: true
>>>>name: soldier-client
>>>>type: SYNC
>>>>sse:
>>>>>connections:
>>>>>>soldier-server:
>>>>>>>url: http://localhost:9526/soldier
>>>>>baidu-map:
>>>>>>>url: https://mcp.map.baidu.com/sse?ak=myKey

**Environment**
SpringBoot:3.4.2
SpringAI: 1.0.0-M8
jdk17
Soldier-server is available normally. After adding baidu-map, an error will be reported when starting the project

comments.body:
This message comes from the underlying java SDK: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/java-sdk

What it does: During connection there's a lock, so you're not able to send a message. If you try to send a message during the connection phase, it waits for 10s. If the connection to the MCP server is not established within that time the error is thrown.

Are you sure that you can connect with your API key to the `baidu-map` endpoint? You can verify this by using the mcp inspector: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector

@denniskawurek I can use Cherry Studio client to connect to this MCP server normally, but I feel that there is a problem with my MCP server path. How should I configure the authentication key? Directly configuring on the URL should be incorrect, do you know?

@hbsjz-swl I don't have access to my computer now, but you could try to use the `connections.[name].sse-endpoint` property like it's described here: https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_sse_transport_properties

Set this value to /sse?ak=abc
And set the URL property to the other value.

But iirc there were recently some changes regarding the logic for the URL builder, so I'm unsure whether it's already release. I think you need to try it out. :)

> [@hbsjz-swl](https://github.com/hbsjz-swl) I don't have access to my computer now, but you could try to use the `connections.[name].sse-endpoint` property like it's described here: https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_sse_transport_properties
> 
> Set this value to /sse?ak=abc And set the URL property to the other value.
> 
> But iirc there were recently some changes regarding the logic for the URL builder, so I'm unsure whether it's already release. I think you need to try it out. :)

Thank you very much for your guidance. I tried it but it still didn't work. I switched to version 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT to solve this problem.

I also tried 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT and that failed as well. I wonder what is different with your test? No matter what I try, it always fails.

> I also tried 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT and that failed as well. I wonder what is different with your test? No matter what I try, it always fails.

Actually, I compiled and tested based on the latest version of the Spring AI source code, so I think this might be equivalent to 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT. I suspect that the issue might not be with the Spring AI code itself, but rather with other external environmental factors.
It is strange - are you using MacOS? I've tried various JDK versions, SNAPSHOT releases, same exception in spring AI codebases. My project dependencies are pretty simple.

> It is strange - are you using MacOS? I've tried various JDK versions, SNAPSHOT releases, same exception in spring AI codebases. My project dependencies are pretty simple. 

Yes，I'm using MacOS，and the JDK version is 17.

Using RC1, I have confirmed the issue only happens when you add this to your gradle:

`implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-tika-document-reader'`

If you remove tika, PDF parsing works. If you add tika, it fails every time.

> Using RC1, I have confirmed the issue only happens when you add this to your gradle:
> 
> `implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-tika-document-reader'`
> 
> If you remove tika, PDF parsing works. If you add tika, it fails every time.

Oh, I see! I tried to write a demo to reproduce the scenario you mentioned, but I still couldn't replicate the issue. I'm using Maven as my build tool, and here are my dependencies: 
```
<dependencies>
     <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-ai-tika-document-reader</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-ai-pdf-document-reader</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>ch.qos.logback</groupId>
        <artifactId>logback-classic</artifactId>
        <version>1.4.14</version>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>
```

 The test code is still the same one we used earlier: 
```
public static void main(String[] args) {
        PdfDocumentReaderConfig readerConfig = PdfDocumentReaderConfig.builder()
                .withPageTopMargin(0)
                .withPageExtractedTextFormatter(
                        ExtractedTextFormatter.builder()
                                .withNumberOfTopTextLinesToDelete(0)
                                .build()
                )
                .withPagesPerDocument(1)
                .build();
        List<Document> pages = new PagePdfDocumentReader("https://www.novo-pi.com/ozempic.pdf", readerConfig).read();
    }
```

yet the PDF conversion continues to work properly. Could it be that you've enabled some additional configuration? This is really strange—I'm completely out of ideas.

My build is straight from start.spring.io using gradle - nothing else.  I'm attaching my sample project that demonstrates the one line fails using no changes from what spring provides.

[spring-ai-pdf-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20225277/spring-ai-pdf-test.zip)

Checking the Unicode string is not empty before accessing first character avoids the `StringIndexOutOfBoundsException`.
 
I encountered this issue after updating to PDF Box 3.0.4 with the pdf in the test case and also for your linked ozempic-pi.pdf. The issue is resolved in both files with the change in the linked PR.

issues.title: MCP Server failed to transport large response to client

comments.body:
Turn out to be an issue with nginx.

Add this header solved the problem
```java
        exchange.getResponse().getHeaders().add("X-Accel-Buffering", "no");
```

```script
curl "http://localhost:8080/chat?userInput=What%20do%20you%20know%20about%20Vaadin%20Dashbord%20component"
```

You can run the same curl in main branch to test that the vaadin mcp works well.

issues.title: VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor not generating default client id
issues.body:
Spring AI version: 1.0.0-M5

As per official docs: 
https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/1.0/api/chatclient.html#_advisor_configuration_in_chatclient

**VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor: Set a default conversation ID to be used if none is provided in the context.**

However, when im trying to run the below code, it throws an error:
`
The conversationId must not be empty!
`

```java
VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor vectorStoreAdvisor = new VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor(
				pgVectorStore,
				conversationId, // sending null from client
				chatRequestDto.chatHistoryWindowSize()
		);
```
Do we have to pass some random conversation id ourself for the first time? It contradicts with the official docs.

What is the right way to implement a conversational API as the conversation id is null for the first time.

comments.body:
@saranshbansal thanks for reporting this issue. It should be fixed in the current version of Spring AI (1.0.0-M8), could you confirm that?

In 1.0.0-M8, the VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor can be instantiated via a Builder object, which configures a default conversation ID if one is not specified at instantiation time.

Thank you Thomas. That's great to hear. I had plans to shift to M8 but since we are so close to 1.0.0, i've held back. 

I'll close the issue.

Not having error and warn logs and random disconnects.

https://github.com/jpomykala/spring-boot-ai-mcp-webmvc-sample

issues.title: spring-ai-bedrock-converse-spring-boot-starter is missing in pring-ai-bom 1.0.0-M8
issues.body:
The dependency 
```
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-ai-bedrock-converse-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
is missing in 
```
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-ai-bom</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0-M8</version>
  <type>pom</type>
  <scope>import</scope>
</dependency>
```

comments.body:
From M6 to M7 there were artifact id changes for the model starters:

spring-ai-{model}-spring-boot-starter → spring-ai-starter-model-{model}

Try this instead

```
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-model-bedrock-converse</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
Thank you @dafriz it worked.

mcp server和 client应该是两个端点，互相调用 并且要有固定的端点配置的 ，如果只用FunctionCalling的话一个服务里边就可以实现，直接在chatClient.prompt().user(String question).tools(YouToolMethodClass).call(); 就可以实现

issues.title: Migrate CassandraChatMemory to CassandraChatMemoryRepository
issues.body:
This would allow us to have a more robust implementation than we currently have based on the ChatMemory.get(conversationId, lastn) interface that is currently deprecated.

This has been done for Jdbc in the PR - as a basis for comparison.

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2890

comments.body:
As for now, cassandraChatMemory stores data in table with 4 columns like that:
```
create table ai_chat_memory (
    session_id        text,
    message_timestamp timestamp,
    assistant         text,
    user              text,
    primary key (session_id, message_timestamp)
)
    with clustering order by (message_timestamp desc);
```

Maybe we should change table structure similar to JdbcChatMemory?
```
create table ai_chat_memory (
    session_id        text,
    message_timestamp timestamp,
    type              text,
    content           text,
    primary key (session_id, message_timestamp)
)
    with clustering order by (message_timestamp desc);
```

It'll give us an ability to store tool/system messages, not only user/assistant.

What do you think @markpollack @michaelsembwever ?

> Maybe we should change table structure similar to JdbcChatMemory?

Then the table would need a primary key like
```
primary key (session_id, message_timestamp, type)
```

Which can be done, and I have nothing against such an approach.

Can't session_id+message_timestamp be a primary key? As far as I remember you set unique timestamp.

On other hand, there is #2902 with request on message id field, so we can introduce it here.
@linarkou , let's create a separate issue for your suggestion, as there's a time-constraint to get this issue fixed.

@markpollack draft PR @ https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3036 
eta to finish it tomorrow.
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3036 is ready for review now.
fantastic!

There's a bug here I need to fix.  @linarkou statement above 

> It'll give us an ability to store tool/system messages

is actually mandatory…  MessageWindowChatMemory relies on persisting SystemMessages.
working on a fix…
Thanks @michaelsembwever

bug (optimisation) fix in progress: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3097 
completion eta ~24 hours.
@michaelsembwever thanks eh!
@michaelsembwever we can always do a bug fix post RC1 pre GA - just want to make sure we avoid modifying and public facing apis/properties.
@markpollack , ready for review https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3097

> we can always do a bug fix post RC1 pre GA 

i don't think before https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3097 it would work at all with MessageWindowChatMemory (as it expected to persist SystemMessages). 

and just generally, the usage pattern of MessageWindowChatMemory was substantially different: and with Cassandra not being a relationship style db; that it really required a revamp of the schema.  #3097 is properly aligned to Cassandra now, provides a number of advantages over other chat-memory implementations (according to its known strengths), and could quite likely outperform latency-wise the other implementations (ofc "it depends").

Thanks so much for the deep analysis and changes at the last minute.

If you want to advertise this in the docs, it may draw more folks into using cassandra.

merged in 87b680af7

issues.title: NumberFormatException when model returns scientific notation (e.g., “1.5009969E7”) for int parameter in tool method

issues.title: Investigate HuggingFace OpenAI API support
issues.body:
The current support for hugging face is based off a code gen solution using swagger to access the custom API that hugging face exposed.  This is different from all other model implementations and as such things like observability are not supported in this model.

As with nvidia, groq, docker model support, etc that expose openai api compatible endpoints, a test class should be created to validate that openai works with hugging face's openai api implementation and the current implementation removed.

comments.body: The investigation found that some of the models hosted, but not all support openai and then the level of the support is rather sporatic.  So we will keep the current minimal swagger based solution.

issues.title: provide setters for the various SQL queries used in the JDBC persistence types
issues.body:
some of the queries that are only available as `private final` fields in the classes are incompatible with certain databases, like Oracle. 

for example `JdbcChatMemory` uses a query with `limit` which wont work on Oracle. 

There's no easy way to fix this short of copy-and-pasting the entire class and excluding Spring AI's solution. if you provide a few setters or make the queries constructor arguments, that would be sufficient to unblock 

please do this for anything that uses JDBC

comments.body: This has been address, the refactoring to focus on `JdbcChatMemoryRepository` and there isn't an impl of `JdbcChatMemory` anymore.  PR #3037 shows how we support multiple dialects.

issues.title: spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-jdbc auto configures the deprecated `JdbcChatMemory`
issues.body: `spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-jdbc` auto configures the deprecated `JdbcChatMemory`. it should, one imagines, auto configure a `MessageWindowChatMemory` with a `JdbcChatMemoryRepository`

comments.body:
It configures a deprecated JdbcChatMemory since we have the M8 release cycle where it is deprecated before removal in RC1.

`MessageWindowChatMemory` is configured in `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory`

Use the starter `spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-jdbc` to get both.
thanks

issues.title: VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor not able to retrieve chat pairs.
issues.body:
Version: 1.0.0.M5

I’m using the vectorstore chat memory advisor to create a conversational Api. 

I’m passing a conversation id in the advisor which stores all chats and responses in my vector table. 

Now, i need a way to retrieve all chats based on given conversation id. The problem is that the response is providing a list of messages for a conversation which i have to manually sort into user and assistant messages.  

Even if i sort them, there seems to be no way to get the chat pairs in response as I can’t find a way to relate which assistant message belongs to which user message.  

Is there a way to implement a chat_pair_id along with timestamps so i can form a proper chat history?

comments.body:
M5 is quite old. 

The new APIs in M8 have a repository implementation to retrieve all messages based on a conversation id.  Messages in a conversation will naturally include user and assistant messages - that is the back and forth interaction.  The new implementation includes a timestamp in  JdbcChatMemoryRepository.

Better docs are coming, but in the meantime you can look in https://github.com/ThomasVitale/llm-apps-java-spring-ai/tree/main/patterns/memory for some good examples.
Brilliant, thanks for that. Looks like a lot of issues in M5 are fixed in more recent versions so i'll plan to move my codebase to M8.

issues.body:
**Bug description**
According to the documentation, you can configure OpenAI's `response_format` through properties ([docs](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_configuring_via_application_properties)). However, using the json schema related properties results in a 400 Bad request error:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Unknown parameter: 'response_format.schema'.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": "response_format.schema",
    "code": "unknown_parameter"
  }
}
```

**Environment**
Using `spring-ai-openai:1.0.0-M8` on Amazon Corretto 21 on macOS M1 Sequoia 15.4.1.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Configure the properties as seen in the documentation:

```java
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.type=JSON_SCHEMA
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.name=MySchemaName
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.schema={"type":"object","properties":{"steps":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"explanation":{"type":"string"},"output":{"type":"string"}},"required":["explanation","output"],"additionalProperties":false}},"final_answer":{"type":"string"}},"required":["steps","final_answer"],"additionalProperties":false}
spring.ai.openai.chat.options.response-format.strict=true
```

2. Run any `ChatClient` prompt, eg:

```java
@Bean
ApplicationRunner runner(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
    ChatClient client = builder.build();
    return args -> {
        String result = client.prompt().user("generate an object").call().content();
        logger.info(result);
    };
}
```

**Expected behavior**
I expect Open AI to return a structured response according to the given JSON schema.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
See code provided in steps to reproduce.

comments.body:
The issue seems to be caused because the `ResponseFormat` class is used both to deserialize the properties as to pass as part of the request body to OpenAI's API. To make that work, the `ResponseFormat.jsonSchema` property is constructed based on the string provided in `ResponseFormat.schema`([relevant code](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/7bb553ecce0a436406a9ed9b091c5d9ae545b9ea/models/spring-ai-openai/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/openai/api/ResponseFormat.java#L91-L96)). 

The result is that both `response_format.json_schema` (good) and `response_format.schema` (bad?) are sent to OpenAI's API.

A workaround is to construct the `ResponseFormat` programmatically, eg:

```java
ChatClient chatClient = builder
	.defaultOptions(OpenAiChatOptions.builder()
		.responseFormat(ResponseFormat.builder()
			.type(ResponseFormat.Type.JSON_SCHEMA)
			.jsonSchema(ResponseFormat.JsonSchema.builder()
				.schema("{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"steps\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"explanation\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"output\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"explanation\",\"output\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},\"final_answer\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"steps\",\"final_answer\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}")
				.strict(true)
				.build())
			.build())
		.build())
	.build();
```

This allows you to construct it without setting the `ResponseFormat.schema` field.

See also:

* [Question asked on Stack Overflow on which I based this issue](https://stackoverflow.com/q/79602663/1915448)
* [Question asked on Reddit (by the same author)](https://www.reddit.com/r/SpringBoot/comments/1kcnebs/in_springai_100snapshot_why_am_i_getting_unknown/)

Great discovery...👍

The `schema` variable should only be used internally within the ResponseFormat class. However, it appears to have been assigned a value other than null, which prevents it from being filtered out by `@JsonInclude(Include.NON_NULL)` (refer to the `setSchema` method).

In my opinion, adding `@JsonIgnore` to the following part should resolve the issue:
```java
private String schema;
```

Would you like to contribute?

I've merged https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2972 and did a brief review on the openai ref docs.  I think this solves the issue.  Thanks!  

If this doesn't resolve the issue, please reopen.

issues.title: PromptChatMemoryAdvisor rerenders prompt, causing template errors
issues.body:
**Bug description**
`PromptChatMemoryAdvisor` seems to call `PromptTemplate.render()` even though the prompt has already been rendered.

**Environment**
This applies to Spring AI M8 (tested on macOS with Amazon Corretto 21). I haven't encountered this issue with Spring AI M7.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Define a `ChatClient` like this:

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(
			new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(MessageWindowChatMemory
				.builder()
				.maxMessages(10)
				.build()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
		.build();
}
```

2. Define `user.st` like this:

```
How would you answer the following question:
{question}
```

3. Now call the following:

```java
String response = chatClient
	.prompt()
	.user(user -> user.param("question", "{,}"))
	.call()
	.content();
```

You now get the following exception:

```

	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.PromptChatMemoryAdvisor.aroundCall(PromptChatMemoryAdvisor.java:90) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]

```

If you put a breakpoint in the code, you can see that `StTemplateRenferer.createST()` is called with a `template` containing: 

```
How would you answer the following question:
{,}
```

My guess is that due to `template` containing an already rendered template, it's interpreting `{,}` as another template literal, which results in invalid syntax.  I also assume this is potentially unsafe, because if the `question` parameter comes from user input, wouldn't they be able to do "stringtemplate injection"?

**Expected behavior**
I don't expect an `IllegalArgumentException` to be thrown. If I roll back to Spring AI M7 and replace the `PromptChatMemoryAdvisor` with the `InMemoryChatMemory`, it works as expected:

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(new InMemoryChatMemory()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
		.build();
}
```

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
See steps to reproduce

comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this issue! In M8, the userText is rendered by `ChatClient` when building the `Prompt` object before calling the advisor chain so to ensure consistency and predictability throughout the execution flow.

The default template engine uses `{}` as delimiters for placeholders to be replaced. If the user text includes any of those characters, they will be considered candidate for templating. If you want to allow a user to use those characters in their question, you can customise the default template engine configuration to use different characters as placeholder delimiters via the new `.defaultTemplateRenderer()` method.

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(
			new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(MessageWindowChatMemory
				.builder()
				.maxMessages(10)
				.build()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
                .defaultTemplateRenderer(StTemplateRenderer.builder().startDelimiterToken('<').endDelimiterToken('>').build())
		.build();
}
```

where `user.st` is changed as follows:

```java
How would you answer the following question:
<question>
```

In alternative, you might be able to keep using the default delimiters, but disable the template validation logic (`ValidationMode.NONE`) or let it log a warning instead of throwing an exception (`ValidationMode.WARN`). Though, I would recommend the previous approach.

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(
			new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(MessageWindowChatMemory
				.builder()
				.maxMessages(10)
				.build()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
                .defaultTemplateRenderer(StTemplateRenderer.builder().validationMode(ValidationMode.NONE).build())
		.build();
}
```

I hope that helps!

Changing the validation mode didn't work for me, but changing the delimiter did.

The reason I noticed it is because one of my parameters contained JSON. So I guess this is also important to take into account when using a structured output, considering that it results in a JSON schema being added to the prompt.

@g00glen00b that's right, when using the output converters via ChatClient, the JSON schema might cause some templating issue if the prompt is rendered again. In the current SNAPSHOT (future RC1), that issue has been solved for the structured output. But it's still something to consider if curly braces are desired in the system or user prompt.

issues.title: Uploading PDF documents as part of the Prompt to OpenAI
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**
Perhaps I am not doing this correctly. If so, please point me in the direction.

OpenAI GPT-4 and on allow you to upload a PDF as part of the chat request. So, something like this should be possible:

```
byte[] fileData = ...
Media media = Media.builder().data(fileData).mimeType(DOC_PDF).build();
Prompt prompt = new Prompt(
        List.of(new UserMessage("extract text from the following PDF document."),
            new UserMessage("some text here", media)), openAiChatOptions);

CallResponseSpec callResponseSpec = chatClient.prompt(prompt).call();
ChatResponse chatResponse = callResponseSpec.chatResponse();
```
**Current Behavior**

While the above compiles, even with GPT-4o (which supports this), I get the following error:

```
org.springframework.ai.retry.NonTransientAiException: 400 - {
  "error": {
    "message": "Invalid MIME type. Only image types are supported.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": null,
    "code": "invalid_image_format"
  }
}
```

<!--- 
How has this issue affected you?
What are you trying to accomplish?
What other alternatives have you considered?
Are you aware of any workarounds?
-->

Pretty clear - can't use a PDF document as part of a UserMessage. I am not currently aware of a workaround, but if there is one, please point me to it.

Much appreciated!

comments.body: > OpenAI GPT-4 and on allow you to upload a PDF as part of the chat request Where did you find this information? Based on the [API specification](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create), it seems that only text, image, and audio inputs are allowed.

I've been looking for information on this and it looks like it's possible using the assistants api.

- related doc
  - https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/assistants
  - https://stackoverflow.com/a/78924474/29672082

Currently, the assistants api is in beta.
As far as I know, spring ai does not support the assistants api yet.

Here is the page that shows you a couple of ways of doing, including the one I was interested in, which is embedding base64 encoded...
https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/pdf-files?api-mode=responses

Under Supported Models it says this:
Only models that support both text and image inputs, such as gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, or o1, can accept PDF files as input.

Thank you for the information.
I think we'd probably need to add a feature to make it work that way
related issue : https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2962

BTW, the assistants api is going to be deprecated.  As per the openai [page](https://platform.openai.com/docs/assistants/overview) 

```
Based on your feedback from the Assistants API beta, we've incorporated key improvements 
into the Responses API. After we achieve full feature parity, we will announce a deprecation
 plan later this year, with a target sunset date in the first half of 2026. 
[Learn more](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/responses-vs-chat-completions).
```

There are some docs on this - https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/multimodality.html#_spring_ai_multimodality

also test case here - https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/bfcaad7b5495c5927a62b44169e8713e044c2497/models/spring-ai-openai/src/test/java/org/springframework/ai/openai/chat/client/OpenAiChatClientIT.java#L308


if that doesn't solve your problem, please reopen the issue.

@markpollack IN the IT that you referenced, this line:
`.media(MimeTypeUtils.IMAGE_PNG, new ClassPathResource("/test.png")`

The `MimeTypeUtils` class doesn't have PDF as an option. Trying to substitute this:
```
.media(MimeType.valueOf("application/pdf"),
                new ClassPathResource("/file.pdf")
```

(`MypeType` is `org.springframework.util.MimeType`)

When I do that, I get the same error response back:
```
org.springframework.ai.retry.NonTransientAiException: 400 - {
  "error": {
    "message": "Invalid MIME type. Only image types are supported.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": null,
    "code": "invalid_image_format"
  }
}
```

Also, I do not have permissions to reopen the issue.

issues.title: [OpenAiApi]- IoException occurs when chatModel response takes too long
issues.body:
**Bug description**
Because the request timeout is not set when creating OpenAiApi#RestClient, when trying to request the AI ​​model provided by a third-party manufacturer, if the request response time is too long, a "ReadTimeOut" exception will occur, but if the request response time is relatively short, it will not occur.

Abnormal origin : org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient.DefaultRequestBodyUriSpec#exchangeInternal

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/89f70f2b-74f4-4ff5-bce4-bef95fd3c7be)

**Environment**
Spring AI 1.0.0-M6
Java version 17
MACOS 15.3.2 (24D81)
﻿
**Steps to reproduce**
1. View OpenAiApi.class source code
Parameters: `org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi#restClient`
Parameters: `org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Builder#restClientBuilder`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b40e77a5-7303-44d2-9614-4c6fb9246b88)

2. Find the method of `OpenAiApi` to build `restClient` attributes
``` java
org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi#OpenAiApi(java.lang.String, org.springframework.ai.model.ApiKey, org.springframework.util.MultiValueMap<java.lang.String,java.lang.String>, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.springframework.web.client.RestClient.Builder, org.springframework.web.reactive.function.client.WebClient.Builder, org.springframework.web.client.ResponseErrorHandler){
this.restClient = restClientBuilder.baseUrl(baseUrl)
			.defaultHeaders(finalHeaders)
			.defaultStatusHandler(responseErrorHandler)
			.build();
}
```

2. Through `org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClientBuilder#build`, the default `ReactorClientHttpRequestFactory` for creating requests is found

3. Parameters: `org.springframework.http.client.ReactorClientHttpRequestFactory#defaultInitializer`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db5d2ad3-db80-46cc-954f-a41eb41f4fc1)

Conclusion: If the response time exceeds 10 seconds, the connection will be disconnected and a response timeout will be prompted.
﻿
**Expected behavior**
I hope that the timeout can be controlled externally when creating an `OpenApi.class`.﻿

comments.body:
The OpenAIApi class has a builder, which allows you to configure a custom REST client with the necessary timeout settings through the builder.

For example:
```java
RestClient.Builder customReadTimeoutRestClientBuilder = RestClient.builder();
customReadTimeoutRestClientBuilder.requestFactory(ClientHttpRequestFactoryBuilder.simple()
		.build(ClientHttpRequestFactorySettings.defaults().withReadTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(30))));

OpenAiApi api = OpenAiApi.builder()
	.restClientBuilder(customReadTimeoutRestClientBuilder)
	.build();
```

(I haven’t verified if this code works. It was written as an example to demonstrate the use of the builder.)
@dev-jonghoonpark Thank you, I'll try it. The documentation doesn't tell me how to use it. Following the example, I only get the default build mode.

issues.title: FunctionCallingOptions was not completely removed
issues.body:
Please do a quick search in GitHub issues first, you may already have a duplicate of the one you are about to create.
If the error is not serious, please create an issue directly. Otherwise, please take a few minutes to fill out the following sections:

**Bug Description**
Due to the changes in PR #2922, the FunctionCallingOptions implemented by MoonshotChatOptions has been removed!

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74fe2e82-094d-416b-96b3-b6e1efa990bf)

**Environment**
Please provide as many details as possible: Spring AI version, Java version, vector storage you use (if any), etc.

**Steps to reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the problem.

**Expected Behavior**
Describe clearly and concisely what you expect to happen.

**Minimum Complete Reproducible Example**
Please provide a failing test or a minimal complete verifiable example that reproduces the issue.

Reproducible bug reports will be resolved before non-reproducible ones.

comments.body: Thanks for raising this issue! The Moonshot integration was moved to the Spring AI Community organization, so this issue should probably be transferred there: https://github.com/spring-ai-community/moonshot
Thank you for your timely response and reminder

issues.title: CRITICAL - Spring AI BOM is missing dependencyManagement section
issues.body:
Last version of 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT in Spring AI BOM is missing dependency section effectively breaking builds trying to fetch latest latest module from the Spring Repo.

**Most likely the cause of the issue is the following commit:

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/acd986e59c8b59ba3fc1c5ec89c75b3db3223f19**

1. removes configuration for flatten-maven-plugin (with custom parameters to preserve dependencyManagment section in the flatted pom)
2. most likely there is another flatten-maven-plugin in the reactor hierarchy that kicks in anyway and removes <dependencyManagement> section completely.

comments.body: Hi. Thanks for the report.  We are in the middle of adoptiong the approach spring cloud has for dependency management, which is that the spring cloud bom does not include any spring boot dependencies.  We will investigate, hang on! ;)

@alexheifetz Sorry about the issue. Fixed via https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/363ebf8de6acd2f6f48e917e9aa27909d32d3066 to keep the right configuration for dependencyManagement. Could you give the latest Spring AI BOM a try (once the current CI build is complete) and let us know how it goes? Thank you
Issue has been resolved. Thanks for prompt response!!

> Hi. Thanks for the report. We are in the middle of adoptiong the approach spring cloud has for dependency management, which is that the spring cloud bom does not include any spring boot dependencies. We will investigate, hang on! ;)


> Hi. Thanks for the report. We are in the middle of adoptiong the approach spring cloud has for dependency management, which is that the spring cloud bom does not include any spring boot dependencies. We will investigate, hang on! ;)

Knowing Dave, there is probably flatten-maven-plugin configuration sitting somewhere in the spring-cloud-build that requires customization at the child level.

issues.title: Support for Huggingface Text Embeddings Inference (TEI)
issues.body:
I am trying to deploy bge embedding models without using ollama for various reasons.

I had one deployed using TEI https://huggingface.co/docs/text-embeddings-inference/local_cpu which, if I understand correctly, is not currently supported by Spring AI natively. 

The other workaround is using spring-ai-starter-model-transformers where I encountered the "Required array size too large" issue of
https://github.com/microsoft/onnxruntime/issues/21321


I wonder if Text Embeddings Inference (TEI) of huggingface will be supported by Spring AI in the future.

comments.body: Never mind. TEI is just another OpenAI Embedding and is already supported.

issues.title: LastMaxTokenSizeContentPurger Logic backwards
issues.body:
I don't see any document references to it and there are no tests so I'm not 100% but looking at LastMaxTokenSizeContentPurger it appears to return all content that will not fit vs the content that does fit

Shouldn't L61 be `newList.addAll(datum.subList(0, index));`  ?
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/ff52859b2d56029dce8f18d47b4fecb9cc8720c4/spring-ai-client-chat/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/client/advisor/LastMaxTokenSizeContentPurger.java#L61C24-L61C31

comments.body:
Hi @jasonparallel,

From what I understand, the loop `while (index < datum.size() && totalSize > this.maxTokenSize)` at line 50 determines the starting point for retaining content, trimming off elements from the beginning of `datum` up to `datum[index - 1]` to stay within `maxTokenSize`.

Given that, `datum.subList(index, datum.size())` at line 61 seems to correctly return the remaining content.

I agree that adding some tests would help confirm this behavior.

If you're not already planning to work on this, would it be alright if I gave it a try?

Could you please share the specific response body?

> com.alibaba.cloud.ai.example.chat.openai.controller.ChatClientController.simpleChat(ChatClientController.java:88) Can you share the code of this part?
> 
> Based on the content of the logs, I think the 400 error might have occurred from the Alibaba API. Could you please share the specific response body?

that is a alibaba simple project example， i use based spring ai to build the chatting ，also  occured  this problem  ，the  code of part ：

ChatClient：

    public static final String DEFAULT_PROMPT = "你好，请吹个牛！";

    public final ChatClient chatClient;


    public CommonController(ChatClient.Builder chatClient) {
        this.chatClient = chatClient
                .defaultAdvisors(new MessageChatMemoryAdvisor(new InMemoryChatMemory()))
                .defaultAdvisors(new SimpleLoggerAdvisor())
                .build();
    }




controller:

    @GetMapping("/simple/chat")
    public String simpleChat() {

        try {
            return chatClient.prompt(DEFAULT_PROMPT).call().content();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            // 记录错误日志
            System.err.println("Failed to call OpenAI API: " + e.getMessage());
            return "Error: " + e.getMessage();
        }
    }

issues.title: chatModel cannot be null when adding SimpleLoggerAdvisor
issues.body:
**Bug description**
When creating a `ChatClient` without explicitly specifying a `ChatModel` (relying on auto-configuration), the call to the LLM fails if a default advisor is set (e.g., `SimpleLoggerAdvisor`). The following exception is thrown:

```
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: chatModel cannot be null
```

**Environment**
- Spring AI version: 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
- Java version: GraalVM 21.0.4 - VM 23.1.4
- Impacted models: VertexAIGemini, Mistral

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Create a ChatClient without specifying a `ChatModel`, using an injected `ChatClient.Builder` and calling `chatClient.build()`.
2. Add a default advisor (e.g., SimpleLoggerAdvisor).
3. Call the LLM, specifying the model via the prompt.

> Note: Everything works as expected if no default advisor is set.

**Expected behavior**
The call to the LLM should succeed, and the advisor should be invoked correctly during execution.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
```kotlin
 package com.malt.platform.spring.test.controller

 import org.springframework.ai.chat.client.ChatClient
 import org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.SimpleLoggerAdvisor
 import org.springframework.ai.chat.prompt.ChatOptions
 import org.springframework.ai.chat.prompt.Prompt
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping
 import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController

 @RestController
 @RequestMapping("/springai-test")
 class SpringAiTestController(
    chatClientBuilder: ChatClient.Builder,
 ) {
    private val chatClient: ChatClient = chatClientBuilder.defaultAdvisors(SimpleLoggerAdvisor()).build()

    @PostMapping("/springai")
    fun springAi(
        @RequestBody prompt: String,
    ): String? =
        chatClient
            .prompt(
                Prompt
                    .Builder()
                    .chatOptions(
                        ChatOptions.builder().model("gemini-2.0-flash-001").build(),
                    ).build(),
            ).user(prompt)
            .call()
            .content()
 }
```

comments.body:
@mautini thanks for reporting this issue. Could you provide some more details about the error, such as the stack trace?

I tried reproducing the error using the same code you shared, but I couldn't reproduce it (using Java).

Here’s a more complete stack trace:

> Current version of Spring AI on main branch.

Are you sure you're in the current version?

in the current version , `spring-ai-core` is not exist since [this commit](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/e3f9b7c16b76f5575567d90904fee1fc4ca74992)

It’s probably because of the following:
> Did a git fetch upstream to update my local cloned repo from several weeks ago, then tried to build.
Great catch! I did a fetch and then confirmed but that's clearly it. I'll review and confirm that all is well in the morning. More news soon.

in [this commit](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/20e2be5578dc75aa2f6276fbe1a592a1e8825eca), `embedding-model-dimensions.properties` file moved from `spring-ai-core` to `spring-ai-model`

If you can’t update to the latest version, you can temporarily pass the test by modifying the value of `originalResourceUri1` variable in `ResourceCacheServiceTests.java` as follows:

```
var originalResourceUri1 = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/resources/embedding/embedding-model-dimensions.properties";
```

issues.title: EPIC - Removing deprecated code
issues.body:
The next release is RC1 which will not have any @Deprecated code.  There is not another M8 to allow users one more release cycle to upgrade.  So some changes such as 


https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/1f59ccadad13f007f07a2389edc33923b6d710ea

wrt to Advisors will be breaking changes.  This needs to be clearly communicate in upgrade-notes.adoc

This is the fine grained list of what deprecated code needs to be removed.


Large Effort

- [x] CallAroundAdvisor needs to be removed and code updated.  So does usage of AdvisedRequest, AdvisedResponse - > use ChatClientRequest and ChatClientResponse  **[Post M8]**


Medium Effort
- [x] Ctors for UserMessage **[Post M8]**
- [x] ChatMemory, InMemoryChatMemory, JdbcChatMemory and JdbcChatMemoryConfig
- [x] All the classes in the model.function package.  This requires minimax and potentially zhipu refactoring. **[In M8]**
- [x] OpenAiApi - ChatCompletionRequest - check about useage of max_tokens, other OpenAI API compatibly libraries may still use it.  **[In M8]**
- [x] https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2518 **[In M8]** 

Low Effort

- [x] Media
- [x] PromptTemplate
- [x] Prompt
- [x] ToolCallingChatOptions
- [x] AnthropicApi
- [x] OllamaApi
- [x] BedrockConverseChatClientIT
- [x] ChatClient, DefaultChatClient
- [x] DocumentRanker, DocumentCompressor and DocumentSelector
- [x] ContentFormatter was listed as deprecated, but we are going to keep it.
- [x] ChatModelObservationContext - remove getRequestOptions **[In M8]** #2518
- [x] Same as above for Embedding and Image modelobservationcontext. **[In M8]** #2518
- [x] ToolCallback **[In M8]** 
- [x] AnthropicApi - left over 'with' Builder methods.
- [x] VertexAiGeminiChatOptions - some simple left over methods.
- [x] Deprecated test methods in ITs, e.g.  BedrockProxyChatModelIT - need to make sure there are equivalents in the updated API.
- [x] SimpleVectorStoreContent




Update examples in our exmaples repo.

- [x] Move to snapshot and rebuilt the examples.

comments.body: Closing this as complete.

issues.title: Remove deprecated code in MiniMax model support
issues.body:
Deprecated code from the switch to the new tool calling infrastructure from the function calling infrastructure need to happen.

Here is the general idea of what needs to happen.

The current design uses the ToolCallingManager to execute the tools. In terms of expected changes,

1) The ChatOptions would extend from ToolCallingChatOptions instead of FunctionCallingOptions
2) The flag proxyToolCalls is replaced by the internalToolExecutionsEnabled to check if the tool execution is handled by Spring AI (this is set to true by default)
3) All the functionCallback options are replaced by toolCallback and toolNames
4) The ChatModel no longer extends from AbstractToolCallSupport but has the toolCallingManager reference to execute the tools
5) There is ToolExecutionEligibilityPredicate which handles the delegation to verify the enabling of tool execution. 

Please take a look at this PR which originally introduced the toolcalling manager support to the models: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/52198ed9d774b0fc983d532fd7b6c582bb003ba9#diff-e5cb77ca54425cc7c57b08f1d70da4118eaf2c0377d381b1d184ace96d6f6e89

comments.body: This is completed via https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/49e1dd10a97e07e3f8997e57f569a0460414982e

Wow, thanks for the reply.  We can move it to spring ai community, but I have no idea how we can maintain it there, if we don't have access to the model.

I heard back from a contact at IBM, what we currently support is the text generation endpoint and that is not where the focus is now. 

"I'd recommend using the chat endpoints (https://www.ibm.com/watsonx/developer/capabilities/chat). Most LLM providers have deprecated their text generation endpoint afaik. "

So I suggest we remove it for now as it isn't what people would really want to use.  If there is interest to create a new module that supports watson chat, that can be taken up separately.

issues.title: mcp-server can not close while mcp-client closed.
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, there might be already a duplicate issue for the one you are about to create.
If the bug is trivial, just go ahead and create the issue. Otherwise, please take a few moments and fill in the following sections:

**Bug description**
mcp-server can not close while mcp-client closed.

**Environment**
```
<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
<artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
<version>3.4.4</version>
<properties>
    <java.version>21</java.version>
    <spring-ai.version>1.0.0-M7</spring-ai.version>
</properties>
```

**Steps to reproduce**
first:
create a mcp-client project, and the content in application.yml:
```
spring:
  ai:
    mcp:
      client:
        stdio:
          servers-configuration: file:d:/mcp_server_config.json
```
the content with mcp_server_config.json:
```
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "spring-ai-mcp-weather": {
      "command": "java",
      "args": [
        "-Dspring.ai.mcp.server.transport=STDIO",
        "-jar",
        "D:\\mcp-server.jar"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```
second:
compiled this project and started it, it started the mcp-server.jar, and works well.

but, when I closed the project, it didn't close the mcp-server.jar, 
and the next time the project can't start mcp-server again, and pending after this message:
```
2025-04-21T20:36:23.925+08:00  INFO 4536 --- [ai-mcp-client] [pool-3-thread-1] i.m.client.McpAsyncClient                : Server response with Protocol: 2024-11-05, Capabilities: ServerCapabilities[experimental=null, logging=LoggingCapabilities[], prompts=null, resources=null, tools=ToolCapabilities[listChanged=true]], Info: Implementation[name=mcp-server, version=1.0.0] and Instructions null
```

**Expected behavior**
I want the mcp-server can be close while mcp-client closed.
And I found Claude-desktop, Cursor can close mcp-server.

comments.body:
Hey @youbl !

I tried recreating a basic setup and have not seen such an issue on MacOS.

I am not sure whether this is an OS-specific problem that you are facing (I noticed you use Windows). Are you able to reproduce this on a Linux/MacOS device?

Regardless of that, if you'd be able to provide some command-line instructions for the workflow you are running (just `mvn` or `java` commands) and a reproducer (e.g. a github repo I can fork) that I can work with, I'd love to help.

@chemicL 
Yes, it works well on Ubuntu, mcp-server will auto close.

this problem only occurs on my windows11,
and I found the cause of this problem, my project added this dependency:
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
if I remove this dependency, the mcp-server will auto close after mcp-client quit.

So it's not an important issue, 
but Claude-desktop and Cursor can close mcp-server, so it's up to you, thanks.

Hi,

I upgraded my project to Spring AI 1.0.0-M7 as I've seen the new tag available, however models starter are not available to this version.

For example :

- same for [spring-ollama-ai-spring-boot-starter](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.springframework.ai/spring-ai-ollama-spring-boot-starter)


Is it still a good practice to include these starters in the pom.xml file? 

And also do you know when the starters will be available for M7?

Thank you

comments.body:
The naming pattern for Spring AI starter artifacts has changed, you should use `spring-ai-starter-model-mistral-ai`.

Reference:
https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/upgrade-notes.html#common-sections
https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/mistralai-chat.html#_auto_configuration
thank you!

issues.title: Spring AI Anthropic Client fails to parse "thinking" content block type from Claude 3.7 response
issues.body:
## Description

The application is failing to process responses from Claude 3.7 Sonnet when using the `thinking` parameter. We're encountering a JSON parsing exception because Spring AI's Anthropic client doesn't recognize the `thinking` content block type in the streaming response.

## Error Details

```
java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to json: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":"","signature":""}}
```

Root cause:
```
com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.InvalidTypeIdException: Could not resolve type id 'thinking' as a subtype of `org.springframework.ai.anthropic.api.AnthropicApi$ContentBlockStartEvent$ContentBlockBody`: known type ids = [text, tool_use]
```

My Code 
```
    val optionsBuilder = AnthropicChatOptions.builder().model(model).temperature(temperature)
        if (thinking) {
            optionsBuilder.thinking(AnthropicApi.ThinkingType.ENABLED, 16000)
            optionsBuilder.maxTokens(24192)
            optionsBuilder.temperature(1.0)
        }

        val prompt = Prompt(messages, optionsBuilder.build())

        return this.chatClient.prompt(prompt).stream().content().collectList().awaitSingle()
            .joinToString(separator = "")
```

Where client is 
```
private val chatClient: ChatClient = ChatClient.create(chatModel)
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure Spring AI with Anthropic client using Claude 3.7 model
2. Enable the "thinking" parameter in `AnthropicChatOptions` using `optionsBuilder.thinking(AnthropicApi.ThinkingType.ENABLED, 16000)`
3. Send a request to the model

## Expected Behavior

The Spring AI Anthropic client should properly parse and handle the "thinking" content block type in the streaming response.

## Environment

- Claude model: claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219
- Java version: 21

## Possible Solution

Update the type registry in Spring AI's Anthropic client to include the "thinking" content block type as a recognized subtype of `ContentBlockBody`. This likely requires adding a class to handle this block type and updating the Jackson deserializer configuration.

issues.title: EPIC - Update 3rd Party Dependencies
issues.body:
Need to update to the latest versions of third party dependencies for vector stores and AI models.


Vector Stores
- [x] Azure Cosmos DB
- [x] Azure Search
- [x] Cassandra
- [x] Chroma
- [x] Coherence
- [x] Couchbase
- [x] Elasticsearch
- [x] Gemfire
- [x] MariaDB
- [x] Milvus
- [x] MongoDB Atlas
- [x] Neo4j
- [x] OpenSearch
- [x] Oracle
- [x] PostgreSQL (pgvector)
- [x] Pinecone
- [x] Qdrant
- [x] Redis
- [x] Typesense
- [x] Weaviate

AI Models

- [x] Amazon Bedrock
- [x] Azure
- [x] Oracle
- [x] Transformers
- [x] Vertex Embedding [Already current - 26.59.0]
- [x] Vertex Gemini [Already current - 26.59.0]

comments.body:
1. Azure Cosmos DB
    - Current: 5.17.1
    - Latest: 5.22.0
  2. Azure Search Documents
    - Current: 11.6.1
    - Latest: 11.7.6
  3. Coherence
    - Current: 24.09
    - Latest: 24.10
  4. Couchbase Client
    - Current: 3.7.8
    - Latest: 3.7.9
  5. OpenSearch Java
    - Current: 2.10.1
    - Latest: 2.23.0 
  6. MariaDB JDBC
    - Current: 3.5.1
    - Latest: 3.5.3
  7. Typesense
    - Current: 0.5.0
    - Latest: 1.3.0
  8. Milvus
    - Current: 2.5.4
    - Latest: 2.5.7
  9. Weaviate
    - Current: 5.0.1
    - Latest: 5.2.0
  10. Redis Jedis
    - Current: 5.1.0
    - Latest: 5.2.0
  11. Neo4j neo4j-cypher-dsl-bom
    - Current: 2024.0.2
    - Latest: 2024.5.1

  The SDKs that appear to be current include:
  - Pinecone
  - SAP HANA
  - pgvector (PostgreSQL)
  - qdrant
  - Elasticsearch (managed by Boot)
  - MongoDB Atlas (managed by Boot)
  - Oracle (managed by Boot)
  - Cassandra (managed by Boot)

AI Model SDK Version Report

  Current vs Latest Versions

  Key Findings

  1. All SDKs need updates: Every third-party AI model SDK is behind the latest version.
  2. AWS Bedrock: Major version update from 2.29.29 to 2.31.23. This is the most significant version leap among all SDKs.
  3. DJL Ecosystem: All DJL components (API, Model-Zoo, HuggingFace Tokenizers, PyTorch Engine) need to be updated from 0.30.0 to 0.32.0. Since they're typically
  updated together, this should be a coordinated update.
  4. Azure OpenAI: Still in beta stage (1.0.0-beta.14 to 1.0.0-beta.16), indicating ongoing development.

  Potential Impact and Considerations

  1. Breaking Changes:
    - AWS Bedrock and Azure OpenAI updates may include API changes as they're still evolving
    - DJL's upgrade from 0.30.0 to 0.32.0 might introduce compatibility issues
  2. New Capabilities:
    - ONNX Runtime 1.21.0 likely includes performance improvements and new operator support
    - Vertex AI updates may add support for newer models like Gemini 1.5
  3. Authentication and API Structure:
    - No indication of authentication method changes in the version updates
    - AWS SDK updates often introduce new service features
  4. Implementation Strategy:
    - Consider updating the DJL ecosystem components together
    - Test AWS Bedrock and Azure OpenAI updates thoroughly due to their beta/evolving status
    - Create integration tests for each updated SDK to verify functionality

  SDKs Using HTTP Clients (No Direct SDK Dependency)

  The following AI models don't use dedicated SDKs but rely on Spring's HTTP clients:
  - Anthropic
  - OpenAI
  - Mistral AI
  - Ollama
  - Stability AI
  - HuggingFace (uses Swagger-generated client from OpenAPI spec)

  These implementations require manual checking of API compatibility with the services.

  Recommended Next Steps

  1. Update Azure OpenAI SDK to 1.0.0-beta.16 [DONE]
  2. Update AWS Bedrock SDK to 2.31.23
  3. Update IBM Watson SDK Core to 9.23.1
  4. Coordinate update of all DJL components to 0.32.0
  5. Update ONNX Runtime to 1.21.0
  6. Check for deprecated methods or API changes in release notes before updating
  7. Run integration tests after updates to verify functionality
Closing this as complete.

comments.body:
已解决，在客户端的yml配置文件中，服务器的地址不要以 / 结尾，否则会请求不到服务器
错误配置：spring.ai.mcp.client.sse.connections.server1.url=http://localhost:8080/
正确配置：spring.ai.mcp.client.sse.connections.server1.url=http://localhost:8080

Translation:

> Solved. In the client's yml configuration file, the server address should not end with /, otherwise the server will not be requested.
Wrong configuration: spring.ai.mcp.client.sse.connections.server1.url=http://localhost:8080/
Correct configuration: spring.ai.mcp.client.sse.connections.server1.url=http://localhost:8080

comments.body:
@HellerZhang Thanks for reporting the issue. How are you using the OllamaOptions here? As long as the prompt options are the instance of ToolCallingChatOptions (via OllamaOptions) the default implementation of the deprecated method is expected to work as in [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/model/tool/ToolCallingChatOptions.java#L80). 

A sample which can help replicate this issue could be helpful.
Irrespective of the above, I have just removed the use of deprecated method in ToolCallingChatOptions [here](15a506985)

我重新查看了我的代码，发现了问题所在，因为我用到了org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.QuestionAnswerAdvisor，所以我手动引入了spring-ai-core，而这个包中正好有一个org.springframework.ai.model.tool.ToolCallingChatOptions，你所修改的spring-ai-model中也有一个org.springframework.ai.model.tool.ToolCallingChatOptions，可能是因为这两个包冲突了。
你有什么建议解决这个问题吗？

You shouldn't be using `spring-ai-core` as spring-ai-core is no longer part of Spring AI since M7 as it has been split into multiple common modules as part of the refactoring. Please read this for more information: https://spring.io/blog/2025/04/04/spring-ai-using-snapshots-part-2

issues.title: If there is no data in the vector database, how do you get the model to answer the question properly
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**

In the use of RetrievalAugmentationAdvisor documenttriiever, the expected behavior
1. If the vector database retrieves the data, answer according to the retrieved data
2. If the vector database does not retrieve the data, answer according to the model itself

**Current Behavior**

Now the situation is that if the vector database has data, the answer is normal, and if the data is not retrieved, the application cannot answer any questions
Can the existing Advisor solve this problem? We look forward to your reply

issues.title: Unable to fetch OpenAI Log Probs when enabled in ChatOptions
issues.body:
**Bug description**
When requesting logprobs generation from OpenAI via `OpenAiChatOptions` the logprobs **are** generated, but never returned as part of the `ChatResponse`.

```
OpenAiChatOptions.builder().logprobs(true).build()
```

**Environment**
Spring AI: 1.0.0-M7
Java: 21

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Configure ChatClient with logprobs enabled in the ChatOptions
2. Make Call to model

**Expected behavior**
Similar to other metadata, logprobs should be returned as part of the metadata.

i.e.
```java
chatClient.call()
				.chatResponse()
				.getMetadata()
				.get("logprobs");
```

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
```java
@Bean
	ApplicationRunner applicationRunner(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
		return args -> System.out.println(builder
				.defaultAdvisors(new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(new InMemoryChatMemory()))
				.defaultOptions(OpenAiChatOptions.builder().logprobs(true).build())
				.build().prompt().system("You are a friendly assistant helping answer questions about pets")
				.user("What is the best pet?")
				.call()
				.chatResponse()
				.getMetadata()
				.keySet());
	}

```

Example Output: 
```
[system-fingerprint, created]
```

issues.title: AnthropicApi does not invoke nullary function tools when using streaming
issues.body:
**Bug description**
When using an Anthropic `ChatModel` with a streaming chat response (`StreamResponseSpec#chatResponse` into `Flux<ChatResponse>`), tools that take in zero parameters are not invoked.

The JSON content returned by Anthropic's text/event-stream response looks like:
```
{"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"msg_01CNBhjoXJNRjoDHX5jLhWa5","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219","content":[],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3154,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":3}}   }
...
{"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Pi8emvHyK6qoLUVjcnmDCr","name":"hello_world","input":{}}            }
{"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":""}       }
{"type":"content_block_stop","index":1   }
```

When parsing the event with type `EventType.CONTENT_BLOCK_STOP`, the `ToolUseAggregationEvent` is not returned due to the `partialJson` being an empty String. In my IDE, this looks like:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/216b3f33-599c-4ca1-b2fd-8c7abd690654)
where you can see the `!eventAggregator.isEmpty()` only passes if `partialJson` has a non-empty value. That's resulting in `StreamHelper#mergeToolUseEvents` returning the `ContentBlockStopEvent` instead of the `ToolUseAggregationEvent` being finished and returned.

In my opinion, either the `isEmpty` check should still return true if the `partialJson` is empty, _or_ if we need to avoid the `squashIntoContentBlock()` call, a different definition should be checked if `eventAggregatgion` applies on `ContentBlockStop` (this would require the `getToolContentBlocks()` check to be modified downstream as well).

**Environment**
spring-ai 1.0.0-M7
Amazon Corretto JDK 21

**Steps to reproduce**
Using Anthropic AutoConfiguration, invoke:
```
chatClient.prompt().user("Call the hello_world tool")).stream().chatResponse();
```
with a simple nullary tool defined:
```
@Tool(name = "hello_world")
String helloWorld() {
	return "Hi spring-ai!";
}
```
Set a breakpoint in that tool, or in StreamHelper as per the above screenshot. You will see that the tool is never invoked.

**Expected behavior**
Nullary tools should still be invoked when streaming -- I note that the tool call works ~normally~ the same as a function with positive arity (still running into https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2670) if I manually set the `partialJson` to `"{}"` in the debugger

This seems similar to this same issue involving Suppliers with Bedrock Converse (https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/1878), though the code is separate. While AnthropicApi has its own StreamHelper class; Bedrock Converse has a similar check for [isEmpty() in ConverseApiUtils](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/v1.0.0-M7/models/spring-ai-bedrock-converse/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/bedrock/converse/api/ConverseApiUtils.java#L296) with its own forked ToolUseAggregationEvent class.

issues.body:

If the bug is trivial, just go ahead and create the issue. Otherwise, please take a few moments and fill in the following sections:

**Bug description**
我用spring ai 实现 mcp server ，如果采用stdio的方式
打成jar包，然后用charry studio 配置，能够正常访问

但是通过spring ai 实现mcp server ，采用sse方式
服务正常启动，但是浏览器死活访问不到sse节点
localhost:8080/sse
通过postman也无法访问（控制台没有任何报错信息）

问题的原因是什么？

**Environment**
pom.xml:

    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-ai-mcp-server-webmvc-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.0-M6</version>
    </dependency>

    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
      <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc</artifactId>
      <version>1.0.0-M7</version>
    </dependency>

**Steps to reproduce**
项目结构非常简单，服务也是正常启动
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4149dde8-b31a-42ec-86aa-6de82c01d1e4)
但是页面上就是一直无法访问：
localhost:8080/sse
通过postman也无法访问

**Expected behavior**
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
Please provide a failing test or a minimal complete verifiable example that reproduces the issue.
Bug reports that are reproducible will take priority in resolution over reports that are not reproducible.

comments.body:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76eeecf1-b11d-4841-9307-c1e9edd0692c)
配置也很简单

就是页面请求不到

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/219d5cc4-07b5-42c2-9df3-a2494ad8daa9)
为什么 1.0.0-M6 和 1.0.0-M7 混用，只用1.0.0-M7不就可以？yml 的配置也没有对齐。

> 为什么 1.0.0-M6 和 1.0.0-M7 混用，只用1.0.0-M7不就可以？yml 的配置也没有对齐。

你好，谢谢答复！
1.版本统一用M6或者M7 ，不混用，还是存在这个问题
2.yml里面，ai.mcp.server不管是从头开始的，还是在spring下面的，也还是存在这个问题。

现在就是服务正常启动，但是启动的日志里面没有类似于tools register success这种，然后页面请求：http://localhost:8080/sse 无法请求到资源

sse use this <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>

服务端用这个

        </dependency>
我试了好几遍 webflux 是会 404  没招
I also encountered this issue, for a while I could successfully access /sse, but after that, I could no longer access it during startup, and it reported an error 404

服务端必须用这个，否则会报404，这是个bug

org.springframework.ai
spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc

I found the cause of the problem, adding the following dependency will not have this issue
```
      <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc</artifactId>
        </dependency>
```
If this dependency is introduced, this error will occur.
```
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux</artifactId>
        </dependency>
```
Although the official documentation recommends using spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux for sse

问题已解决，就是用[kkxrrh](https://github.com/kkxrrh)的方法，pom.xml删除webflux依赖就可以了。

这应该是官方的问题，也不清楚为啥有这个问题，官方还要推荐webflux
+1   WTF

Springboot  Version: 3.1.0
Dependency: 
```xml
<dependencyManagement>  
    <dependencies>       
	    <dependency>          
		    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>  
	        <artifactId>spring-ai-bom</artifactId>  
	        <version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>  
	        <type>pom</type>  
	        <scope>import</scope>  
	    </dependency>    
    </dependencies>
</dependencyManagement>

<dependencies>
  <dependency>  
      <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>  
      <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux</artifactId>  
  </dependency>
</dependencies>
```

本人实测 Webflux 模式也可以用，并且可在 Cherry Studio 中可用，但是必须要在 application.yaml 或者 bootstrap.yaml中加上这样一个配置

I have test it on my own with webflux version of mcp-server dependency, also available for SSE in Cherry Studio. But you must add this property to your springboot application:

```yaml
spring:
  main:
    web-application-type: reactive
```
我猜原因是因为 webflux 是响应式的（非阻塞的），而正常情况下是 MVC （阻塞式），因此 webflux 不可用

I guess the reason is that webflux is reactive (ASYNC), while under normal circumstances it is in the MVC style (SYNC), so webflux is not available

不过最终本人采用的还是 spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc，原因是我发现设置 reactive 后 OpenFeign Client 不可用。这里我猜想 FeignClient 是 MVC 阻塞式的

However, in the end, I still adopted spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc. The reason is that I found that OpenFeign Client was unavailable after setting reactive. Here I suspect that FeignClient is MVC SYNC.

另外，采用 webmvc 会导致 Springboot-Gateway 转发不可用，因为我的 Springboot-gateway 是 reactive 的。本问题待解决

In addition, adopting webmvc will cause Springboot-Gateway forwarding to be unavailable because my Springboot-gateway is reactive. This problem remains to be solved.

issues.title: How to use dynamic api keys with openAI
issues.body: I am trying to use Spring AI in a multi-tenant application. The openAI api key needs to be set on a per request basis according to our user context. I think this functionality was added in https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2164 however the [documentation](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/1.0/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_api_key_management) uses the `OpenAiChatClient` class which I cannot find in milestone 7 (appears to be gone after 0.8.1?). How can I set the api key for chat and embedding requests?

comments.body:
Yikes, many mistakes on my part here, apologies.

The docs are wrong, there was never such a class.  The `OpenAiChatAutoConfiguration` is not allowing one to override the use of `SimpleApiKey`.

Unfortunately, the `@Bean` definition that creates `OpenAiChatModel` contains many arguments, but that would seem to be the best way to add in your own implementation of the `ApiKey interface`, by basically reproducing what is here

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/3756e165262f74e11ab12608b9585a4cf31bc6e0/auto-configurations/models/spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-openai/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/model/openai/autoconfigure/OpenAiChatAutoConfiguration.java#L72


 I realize that isn't convenient.   I think that the `OpenAiChatModel` should have some sort `mutate()` method (like in RestClient) so that you can get the autoconfigured one and then change a few details such as the implementation of the `ApiKey` interface used.
Thanks for the reply. A `mutate()` style pre-initialized builder would be just great.
#3037 Should help, we are merging it into RC1. Closing this issue, please reopen if that solution doens't work for you.  Thanks!

issues.title: Support for Azure OpenAI Structured Outputs feature
issues.body:
Support for the structured outputs feature in Azure OpenAI ensures that the messages generated by the model adhere to a specified schema.

In OpenAiChatOptions, we can achieve this using:

```java
OpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(new ResponseFormat(ResponseFormat.Type.JSON_SCHEMA, "json_schema_here"))
                .build();
```
https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_structured_outputs

However, in AzureOpenAiChatOptions, the only available option is to generate generic JSON using:

```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(AzureOpenAiResponseFormat.JSON)
                .build();
```

Alternatively, you can instruct the model via a prompt, which is not a reliable approach.

Here is the documentation for the Azure GA API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference#responseformatjsonschema

**Expected Behavior**

```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(new ResponseFormat(ResponseFormat.Type.JSON_SCHEMA, "json_schema_here"))
                .build();
```

**Current Behavior**

The structured outputs feature in Azure OpenAI cannot be utilized.

```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(AzureOpenAiResponseFormat.JSON)
                .build();
```

Am I missing something, or is this feature simply not implemented?

comments.body:
There is a PR for it now that will be reviewed, however ,what remains is improving the `BeanOutputConverter` to not append the text to "beg it to return json" and rely on the response format to do the right thing.

Closing this issue as it is a duplicate.   Please reopen if I made a mistake.

issues.title: Make is easier to select either openai or azure openai, ideally heuristically

comments.body: I will look into this as part of a larger issue for creating many instances of many chat models.  That said, I'm not that keen to ensure we use the same environment variables as that in other ecosystems.

> That said, I'm not that keen to ensure we use the same environment variables as that in other ecosystems.

ok mainly if it is at least the same cardinality and shape will be helpful. For example mechanically rewriting openai ENV to spring's conventions. Thanks for considering @markpollack!

There likely isn't a single standard in python land for all the env-vars that can be translated to spring's property format.  It isn't a path I really want to pursue.

Maybe i'm just tired, but can you explain a bit more clearly what behavior you are looking for

`Specifically, if AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY is non empty, don't export a bean for azure,`

this seems counter intuitive to me.

There are projects such as https://github.com/pivotal-cf/java-cfenv/tree/main/java-cfenv-jdbc that do some conversion in a perhaps more reusable manner.

I need only one line to swap azure for normal openai in python and the `AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY` is reliable as it is canonical.
```python
    openai_client = AsyncAzureOpenAI() if os.getenv("AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY") else None
    model = OpenAIProvider(openai_client=openai_client, use_responses=False).get_model(model_name)
```

I would be easy to heuristically configure azure openai vs normal openai depending on which ENV are defined. I would like to have both azure and openai in the same dependency as sometimes the same app needs to switch between one or the other depending on dev vs prod. Ideal case is auto-configuration using canonical ENV variables defined by openai.

If not keen, please close the issue, I mainly raised this to give a chance for it to be addressed.
